problem_addressed: Current machine learning potentials lack reliable uncertainty quantification, limiting their use in active learning, calibration, and out-of-distribution detection.
core_claim: Bayesian E(3)-equivariant MLPs with iterative restratification and joint energy-force uncertainty modeling achieve competitive accuracy while enabling uncertainty-guided applications.
key_contributions: Development of Bayesian E(3)-equivariant MLPs with iterative restratification of many-body message passing
Introduction of joint energy-force negative log-likelihood (NLLJEF) loss function for uncertainty modeling
Systematic benchmarking of multiple Bayesian approaches (deep ensembles, SWAG, IVON) on uncertainty tasks
Demonstration of efficient active learning using BALD with energy-force uncertainty quantification
novelty: Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling via NLLJEF loss enables Bayesian models to match deterministic model accuracy while providing reliable uncertainty estimates, closing the performance gap between Bayesian and conventional approaches.
method_summary: They develop Bayesian E(3)-equivariant MLPs using iterative restratification of many-body message passing, trained with a joint energy-force negative log-likelihood loss function that explicitly models uncertainties in both energies and forces.
method_components: Bayesian neural network architecture with E(3)-equivariance
Iterative restratification of many-body message passing
Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling via NLLJEF loss
Multiple Bayesian approaches (deep ensembles, SWAG, IVON, LLPR)
key_innovation: The NLLJEF loss function that simultaneously optimizes for energy and force uncertainties, reducing energy errors by 3-4 times and force errors by 2-3 times compared to energy-only uncertainty modeling while maintaining competitive accuracy.
assumptions: Joint modeling of energy and force uncertainties is more effective than energy-only uncertainty modeling
E(3)-equivariance is essential for physical consistency in interatomic potentials
Bayesian approaches can provide practical uncertainty estimates for active learning applications
theoretical_grounding: No explicit proofs or bounds provided; empirical validation through extensive benchmarking on oBN25 and 3BPA datasets demonstrating improved calibration, OOD detection, and active learning performance.
hyperparameters: λE and λF for energy/force loss weighting; 2 or 8 Bayesian output heads
title: Bayesian E(3)-Equivariant Interatomic Potential with Iterative Restratification of Many-body Message Passing
